codeReview.summary: The implementation fully satisfies all feature requirements. The run_convergence_analysis.R script (512 lines) correctly runs both single-stage DLNM and two-stage DLNM+mixmeta on the SAME truth data file, computes all required statistical accuracy metrics (bias, MSE, RMSE, relative bias, CI coverage), records numerical convergence (iterations, converged flag, timing), and saves results to three CSV files with the exact column structure specified. The script ran successfully at both 10MB and 100MB scales with C2 config. Tests cover unit verification of metric computations and integration verification of CSV output structure. All 226 tests pass.

issues.description: Package installation within analysis script could be extracted to init.sh or made conditional on CI. Currently installs packages on every run if missing, which may not be desired in all environments.

Mixmeta fallback from REML to ML is good defensive coding, but there's no fallback logged to the CSV output - users might not know which method was actually used if they only look at CSVs. Consider adding a 'method' column to numerical_convergence.csv.

Integration tests skip if CSV files don't exist. While this is appropriate for isolated test runs, it means CI might pass even if the convergence analysis never ran. Consider adding a separate workflow that ensures analysis runs before tests.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker discovered that R's integer() function with data.table loaded may produce unexpected length-1 vectors. Used rep(0L, n) as workaround. This data.table + base R interaction quirk is not documented in AGENTS.md.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows debugging of 'names attribute [24] must be the same length as the vector [1]' error. Worker found integer(n_cities) returned length-1 vector and switched to rep(0L, n_cities).

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker noted that crossreduce() centering messages ('centering value unspecified') appear in verbose output. The analysis-worker skill does not mention this expected behavior, which could cause confusion.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoff.skillFeedback.suggestedChanges: 'The analysis-worker skill could mention that crossreduce() centering messages are expected when not providing a cen argument, to avoid confusion in verbose output.'

sharedStateObservations.observation: Single-stage DLNM produces very low CI coverage (2%) compared to two-stage mixmeta (26-76%) because single-stage pools all cities into one GLM with conditional SEs, while two-stage properly accounts for between-city heterogeneity. This is expected statistical behavior documented in the handoff but not in AGENTS.md or the analysis-worker skill.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: convergence_metrics.csv shows ci_coverage: single_stage=0.02, two_stage=0.76 at 10mb. Handoff explains this is expected - not a bug.

summary: Feature 'run-comparison-analysis' passes code review. The implementation correctly compares single-stage optimized DLNM vs two-stage DLNM+mixmeta on shared truth data, computing bias, MSE, RMSE, relative bias, and CI coverage metrics. All three CSV outputs (convergence_metrics.csv, convergence_details.csv, numerical_convergence.csv) have the expected columns and valid data. Both 10MB and 100MB C2 runs completed successfully. The 50 new test cases verify metric computation and CSV structure. Three non-blocking observations: (1) package installation in script could be externalized, (2) mixmeta method used not logged to CSV, (3) integration tests skip if CSVs don't exist. Worker followed the analysis-worker skill procedure correctly and provided useful feedback for skill improvement.
